pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 51, 57, 69, 88 - all MySQL operations target DrTuxTest database
setup_task.sh lines 77-85 - helper file created at exact path with CSV format: GUID,FirstName
setup_task.sh line 92 - file path referenced for cleanup; export_result.sh line 59 - file checked for existence

setup_task.sh lines 26-31: 5 patients generated (GUID1-GUID5). Lines 67-74: Only 2 inserted into IndexNomPrenom (MARTIN=GUID2, LEROY=GUID4). Therefore 3 orphaned: DUPONT (GUID1), BERNARD (GUID3), MOREAU (GUID5)

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic MySQL database named 'DrTuxTest' containing randomly-generated patient test data. The setup creates 5 fake patient records (DUPONT, MARTIN, BERNARD, LEROY, MOREAU) with fabricated addresses, phone numbers, and SSNs. Exactly 3 patients are intentionally left orphaned by inserting only 2 of the 5 into the IndexNomPrenom search index. A helper CSV file at /home/ga/orphan_firstnames.csv maps the randomly-generated UUIDs to first names. The task requires repairing all 3 orphaned records and writing a reconciliation report to /home/ga/orphan_repair_report.txt.
